paper_title: Ramsey multiplicity and the Turán coloring
summary: No resolution of the conjecture has been found in the literature. The obstacle noted in the source paper remains: $r(k)$ is known only for $k=3$ and $k=4$, and for both values the Ramsey coloring of $K_{r(k)-1}$ is unique with all vertices of equal red and blue degree, which prevents the approach of Lemma 5.2 from working. A direct follow-up (arXiv:2309.06959) extends the Fox–Wigderson Ramsey multiplicity framework to off-diagonal variants but does not appear to address Conjecture 5.3 on mixed blowup colorings.
search_queries: Fox Wigderson "Ramsey multiplicity" "Turán coloring" "mixed blowup" arXiv 2207.07775
"mixed blowup coloring" "Ramsey multiplicity" graph coloring conjecture
notes: No follow-up paper was found that directly addresses Conjecture 5.3. The related paper arXiv:2309.06959 ('Turán Colourings in Off-Diagonal Ramsey Multiplicity', Electronic Journal of Combinatorics) extends the Fox–Wigderson Ramsey multiplicity work to off-diagonal variants but does not discuss mixed blowup colorings. The conjecture is also intrinsically hard because progress depends on knowledge of $r(k)$ beyond $k=4$, which remains open.
